reasoning: The Boston Celtics have secured a top seed (likely #2 behind Cleveland) in the Eastern Conference and rested their top 6 players for the regular season finale, indicating they enter the playoffs mostly healthy. Betting markets in late March listed their championship odds around +225 (implied probability ~30.7%), which generally translates to roughly a 55-60% chance to win their conference, factoring in a competitive but slightly less top-heavy East. This closely mirrors the Polymarket probability of 56% observed on April 3. With no major roster changes or sudden injuries heading into the start of the playoffs on April 19, there is no strong reason to deviate from the 56% established baseline.
